title: Director playability sprint
summary: Park the next two-lane sprint behind beta integration signoff, with handheld readability as the first gate before new breadth.

execution_order.goal: Scope lane 1's handheld readability repair contract from the director browser proof.
execution_order.why_now: The station/title/journal surfaces are the only obvious playability blockers after the beta expansion wave.

execution_order.goal: Repair the filed Source to Shore station, title prompt, and journal route-card readability without changing progression.
execution_order.why_now: The next breadth wave should start from a clean handheld surface.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 1's native browser proof and decide whether the visual gate is closed.
execution_order.why_now: Visual clarity is the director-level gate for opening parallel breadth.

execution_order.goal: Scope lane 2's compact atlas/archive source-risk contract.
execution_order.why_now: Content richness should focus on payoff and source safety, not more surface density.

execution_order.goal: Add selected sketchbook/archive payoff for route-defining entries only.
execution_order.why_now: Small archive rewards improve replay without widening station or journal layout.

execution_order.goal: Add one high-value close-look payoff if the scout confirms source and copy fit.
execution_order.why_now: One visual content reward is safer than another broad text pack.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 2 for science, copy budgets, and archive/journal crowding.
execution_order.why_now: Content density must remain kid-readable at handheld scale.

execution_order.goal: Proof lane 2's tundra snow-edge and meltwater wayfinding before adding any cue.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 3's next value is clarity, not more solved forest density.

execution_order.goal: Implement one tiny lane-2 tundra-local relief cue only if proof finds a gap.
execution_order.why_now: Any spatial fix should be smaller than a traversal framework change.

execution_order.goal: Consolidate lane 2 proof for the full forest giant-tree loop and add only one local nudge if needed.
execution_order.why_now: The forest vertical family is dense enough that proof-only closure may be success.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 2 vertical proof, prompt competition, recovery paths, and lane boundaries.
execution_order.why_now: Vertical clarity can degrade quickly at 256x160 if cues stack.

execution_order.goal: Define lane 1's post-Source-to-Shore route-family boundary before any playable route expansion.
execution_order.why_now: Filed Dune Catch must stay terminal until a route-family handoff is designed.

execution_order.goal: Extract lane 1 route replay-note resolution from the station board into a small route-owned helper.
execution_order.why_now: Board assembly is absorbing lane 4 route semantics and should be decoupled before breadth.

execution_order.goal: Proof lane 1 filed Source to Shore revisit memory without reopening an active route.
execution_order.why_now: Filed memories may need a quiet payoff, but not a fourth beat or new route state.

execution_order.goal: Review lane 1 route-loop cohesion across active, ready, filed, replay, and support states.
execution_order.why_now: Lane 4 should close with route boundaries clear before anyone adds a new route.

completion_gate: Packet 182 is closed or explicitly promotes a blocker from this packet.
Native 256x160 browser proof shows no critical title, station, journal, or route-card overlap.
No new playable route, station page, save schema, content sprawl, traversal framework, inventory, or planner lands in this sprint.
guardrails: Packet 182 remains the immediate gate before this sprint starts.
Treat handheld readability as a playability blocker, not optional polish.
Lane 1 owns station, progression, route, support, replay, and integration-proof work.
Lane 2 owns content, science, atlas/journal, close-look, sketchbook, spatial, and vertical work.
Lane 2 does not edit station, route, map, save, or overlay layout without a lane 1 handoff.
Use xhigh only for lane 1, director work, or unexpected cross-lane blockers; lane 2 should normally run high.

lane_effort.lane-1: xhigh during packet 182 or critical readability repair, otherwise high

gate_after_packet_182.decision: Packet 182 closed with a confirmed native 256x160 readability blocker.
notes: Promote lane 1's handheld readability repair contract first.
Keep lane 2 content and spatial breadth parked until the lane 1 repair is reviewed cleanly.

scout_01_contract.decision: Promote the lane-1 handheld readability repair as the next active implementation step.

repair_surfaces.surface: Filed Source to Shore / Dune Catch station routes page
repair_surfaces.target: Separate station header, route board, archive/atlas note, and support rows while keeping the route title, FILED state, three filed beats, and selected support visible.
Show dune grass, lee cover, and wrack line without single-line truncation.
Keep the route title, progress label, and summary readable inside the card without right-edge clipping.
Keep the bottom start hint and START/MENU buttons readable without lower-edge crowding.

minimum_verification: npm run test -- src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/journal-selector.test.ts
native 256x160 browser proof with paired state JSON and browser-errors.json
npm run validate:agents after queue or packet edits
scope_exclusions: new route ids or playable route breadth

main_01_result.decision: Handheld readability repairs are implemented and ready for critic gate review.
verification: npm run test -- src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/journal-selector.test.ts
native 256x160 browser proof with paired state JSON and browser-errors.json
shared web-game client proof for the first playable notice frame

critic_01_result.decision: Clean handheld readability gate; packet 192 may open the next minimal lane-parallel scout steps.
verification: reviewed five native 256x160 proof PNGs and paired state JSON files
browser-errors.json reports zero errors and zero warnings
npm run test -- src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/journal-selector.test.ts
scope_confirmed_unchanged: filed Source to Shore remains complete
no new route ids or route breadth
no content packs or new journal surfaces

scout_02_contract.decision: Promote a content-only sketchbook archive parity pass for selected route-memory entries.
scope_exclusions: new archive panel or journal surface
route filed text or support behavior
new inspectables or broad content packs

main_02_result.decision: Content-only sketchbook archive parity pass landed for the selected route-memory entries.
scope_confirmed_unchanged: route filed text and support behavior

main_03_result.decision: Single close-look payoff landed for coyote-brush through the existing close-look seam.

critic_02_result.decision: Clean content richness science signoff for sketchbook archive parity and coyote-brush close-look payoff.

scout_03_result.decision: Proof-only closure is enough for Tundra Reach snow-edge, snow-meadow, thaw-skirt, and meltwater-edge wayfinding; no optional tundra-local cue is needed.
scout_03_result.recommended_main_04_mode: proof-only no-runtime closure using the scout proof artifacts
verification: native 256x160 browser proof with paired state JSON and browser-errors.json

main_04_result.decision: Closed the tundra relief clarity pass as proof-only with no runtime, geometry, cue, route, station, save, or content changes.
verification: reviewed native 256x160 browser proof from ECO-20260514-scout-03
reviewed browser-errors.json with zero console or page errors

main_05_result.decision: Closed the forest giant-tree loop as proof-only; existing native browser proof plus current focused tests cover the lower cave, high-run, trunk-foot, crown/rest, branch-nursery, and return-snag loop.

critic_03_result.decision: Clean vertical sprint closure for lane 2; tundra and forest proof-only passes are accepted with no follow-up implementation.
critic_03_result.lane_2_next: parked until another lane-2 item is promoted

scout_04_contract.decision: Keep filed Source to Shore terminal after Dune Catch and promote only a behavior-preserving route replay-note helper extraction.
allowed_after_filed_dune_catch: resolveSourceToShoreFiledArcCopy station, atlas, archive, expedition subtitle, card, and filed-notice copy
existing filed station support rows without active-outing implication
main_06_scope: extract getReplayNote/applyReplayNote behavior from field-season-board into a small route-owned helper
preserve FieldSeasonBoardState shape and all player-facing copy
keep field-request-state world-map replay labels unchanged
keep game.ts biome-enter replay notices unchanged
prove filed Source to Shore remains complete with replayNote null
scope_exclusions: route marker target after filed Dune Catch
new routeV2Progress after filed Dune Catch

main_06_result.decision: Extracted route replay-note resolution and active-beat decoration from field-season-board into field-season-replay-notes without behavior changes.
source_to_shore_boundary: targetBiomeId remains null after filed Dune Catch
activeBeatId remains null after filed Dune Catch
replayNote remains null after filed Dune Catch
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "replay|Source to Shore|Dune Catch|route-marker|filed"

main_07_result.decision: Closed filed Source to Shore revisit proof as proof-only; existing quiet memory notices cover Treeline, Forest, and Coastal Scrub without reopening active route state.
verification: native browser proof with screenshots, state JSON, assertions.json, and browser-errors.json
npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch|memory notices|memory pockets|filed"

critic_04_result.decision: Clean lane-1 route-loop cohesion signoff; packet 192 has no remaining active lane-1 or lane-2 queue item.
verification_reviewed: npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Dune Catch|route-marker|replay|filed|memory notices|memory pockets|High Pass filed|debug save snapshots"
scope_confirmed_unchanged: route marker target after filed Dune Catch

post_packet_path: If clean, activate the parked lane items with one runner in lane 1 and one runner in lane 2.
If the visual gate fails, promote lane 1's repair before opening lane 2 breadth.
